Protein Half Lives in Renal mpkCCD Epithelial Cells (Single-Point Method)

Brief methods:      This method compares the abundances of light and heavy forms of tryptic peptides as a function of time after switching from the abundant (light) form of lysine and arginine to heavy lysine and arginine [substitution of 13C for 12C and 15N for 14N] at time zero. In some experiments, the switch was from heavy to light lysine and arginine. The steady state is maintained and consequently the method does not require cycloheximide treatment of cells.      Half lives were determined with a single time point method using the equation: t1/2 = ts[ln2/ln(1 + Ψ)]. Where ts is the sampling time, Ψ is the heavy-to-light ratio for each peptide in the MS1 spectrum. This equation is based on steady-state assumption and first-order kinetics (verified experimentally using Multi-Point Method). Website created by Pablo Sandoval, Fahad Saeed, Trairak Pisitkun, and Mark Knepper. Contact: knep@helix.nih.gov Reference: P.C. Sandoval, D.H. Slentz, Trairak Pisitkun, Fahad Saeed, J.D. Hoffert and M.A. Knepper. Proteome-wide measurement of protein half lives and translation rates in vasopressin-sensitive collecting duct cells. Journal of the American Society of Nephrology 24:1793-805, 2013.

Database updated: December 19, 2011 (download data)

Click here to see the Multi-Point Method Database. Click here to see the Abundance and Relative Translation Rate Database.

Sort database by:

 * :  Protein identified in less than 3 samples.  n/a :  Protein not identified in this condition.  t½ : Mean ± standard error
